name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface

summary.name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
summary.hypothesis: Round 26 answered the main question about the grouped-row non-PTX sibling: it is a stable alternate surface, but it does not break the plateau. Runtime recovered from 24.690687 ms to 24.183295 ms, yet the machine state came back almost exactly to the same 48.34%-tensor / 16.61%-warps / 5.49%-barrier / 7.23%-long-scoreboard envelope. That means the best next move is to restore the exact PTX winner again so the loop re-centers on the strongest measured anchor before it spends more rounds on tertiary surfaces.
summary.expected_bottleneck: No new bottleneck is being attacked here; this is the exact recovery path back to the best measured correct surface.
summary.risk: Low. This is the exact best measured surface and the safest way to erase the residual gap between the current alternate surface and the recorded winner.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: This now ranks first because both the auxiliary 256x128 probe and the non-PTX sibling have failed to open a new machine-state regime.
The frontier also agrees: after the round-26 measurement, the exact PTX restore is again the highest-priority live representative.
The right use of round 27 is to re-anchor on the cleanest measured winner before spending more budget on lower-confidence branches.
summary.stop_condition: Stop once the exact PTX winner surface is restored or if a remeasurement shows that it no longer reproduces the prior 24.16-24.18 ms band.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
